retrieval_text: Wild Lettuce is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 40 works and 81 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Wild Lettuce. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Berry, Flower, Leaf, Bark, Seed. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Juice, Syrup, Tincture, Extract, Powder. Common condition co-mentions include Wounds or ulcers, Cold or catarrh, Inflammation, Dropsy, Cholera, Rheumatism. Caution co-mentions include Narcotic or sedative caution, Dose caution, Child-use caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include An improved system of domestic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on anatomy and physiology, the practice of medicine, with a copious materia medica , and an extensive treatise on midwifery, embellished with over one hundred useful engravings, gotten up expressly for family use; An improved system of domestic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on anatomy and physiology, the practice of medicine, with a copious materia medica, and an extensive treatise on midwifery, embellished with over one hundred useful engravings, gotten up expressly for family use / by Horton Howard.; An improved system of botanic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on the practice of medicine with a copious materia medica / by Horton Howard.; An improved system of botanic medicine : founded upon correct physiological principles : comprising a complete treatise on the practice of medicine with a copious materia medica. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
